statement: This record remains negative evidence on build 26100: the repo did not produce enough supporting proof to promote it into a normal actionable surface.
gating_reason: This record is cross-layer verified and also aligned with a shipped one-click surface.

links.title: Microsoft Learn: Defender Policy CSP SubmitSamplesConsent
links.summary: Microsoft maps SubmitSamplesConsent to HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows Defender\Spynet and documents 0 = Always prompt, 1 = Send safe samples automatically, 2 = Never send, and 3 = Send all samples automatically.

links.title: Microsoft Learn: Block at First Sight dependency on sample submission
links.summary: Microsoft states that if the sample-submission setting is 2 (Never send), the Block at First Sight feature will not function.

links.title: Local Defender sample-submission lead note
links.summary: The local lead note tracks SubmitSamplesConsent on HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows Defender\Spynet with 0, 1, 2, and 3 as the documented values.

links.title: Windows Defender dump list includes SubmitSamplesConsent
links.summary: The local Defender dump list includes SubmitSamplesConsent under the Defender policy surfaces tracked from upstream registry material.

links.title: Win25H2Clean absent-value check for Defender sample submission
links.summary: In the clean high-risk snapshot run where only SpyNetReporting = 2 was written on the same Defender Spynet policy branch, SecurityHealthService.exe queried SubmitSamplesConsent and got NAME NOT FOUND.

links.title: Win25H2Clean Procmon read for SubmitSamplesConsent = 2
links.summary: After writing SubmitSamplesConsent = 2 on the Defender Spynet policy path, SecurityHealthService.exe queried the same value and read Data: 2 directly from the policy branch.
